# Reduction

step 1: write in input and goal (normally with constrainst)
step 2: To prove the problem is in NP
show there is an algorithm V that can prove the constraints satisfied in polynomial time
step 3: prove the problem is NP-Hard by providing a reduction from xxx(a famous NP problem)
xxx problem A $_p\le$ problem B
==If you have the black box for B, you can solve A in polynomial time==
A --> B: show the construction process follow the restraints
B --> A

## NP problems
1. Subset sum
Input: a set of integer; target T
Goal: a subset that its sum is target T

3-SAT to 4-SAT
2-colored graph to 3-colored graph
